keyRules: Max. 120 m above ground level (AGL) in the open category
Registration for items weighing 250 g or more (or less than 250 g if photographed) — LBA registration number
LBA operator registration is subject to a fee — minimum age is 16. The registration number (e-ID) must be clearly visible on the drone.
A1/A3 Remote Pilot Certificate (fee-based online test administered by the LBA) or A2 Certificate for flying closer to people — check current fees at lba.de
Mandatory Liability Insurance (Section 43 of the German Aviation Act) — including for recreational drones
Prohibition on flying over residential properties without the residents' consent (Section 21h of the Air Traffic Regulations)
Within 100 meters of railroad facilities, highways, or industrial facilities without the consent of the operator or owner
Flight Ban in Nature Reserves and National Parks
ED-R 146: Permanent Restricted Zone over Berlin (Government District) — Permission Required
Direct Remote ID has been mandatory EU-wide for C1, C2, and C3 class drones since January 1, 2024
importantNotes: dipul shows airspace classes, but not a complete overview of restricted zones — supplement with the Droniq app
Foreign pilots with EU registration must still comply with national aviation regulations
Taking photos on private property can also raise GDPR issues

fields.disclaimerText: Always check official sources before your flight. This app is a tool—not a legal reference. Rules are subject to change without notice.
